requiredFiles.reason: Add the accessible spoken name to the existing #watchIncident star button without changing its markup-dependent behavior.
Add an independent regression test in the documented generated-test location for the button's accessible name and preserved watch behavior.

acceptanceCriteria: The existing star button that watches the incident has the spoken accessible name "Watch incident".
Pressing the star button must continue to toggle data-watching from "false" to "true" and update the status text to "Watching incident".
Changes must remain scoped to the reported behavior.
Frontend regressions may use tests.frontend_harness.run_dashboard with the complete boot-time DOM fixture.
Generated regressions belong under tests/generated/test_*.py and use the standard-library unittest runner.
implementationChecklist: Add an accessible naming attribute to the existing button with id="watchIncident" whose value is exactly "Watch incident".
Keep the star glyph visible and retain the existing button id, type, data-watching state, click handler integration, and live status behavior.
Add a generated unittest that verifies the watch button's accessible-name markup is exactly "Watch incident".
In the generated test, invoke the existing dashboard harness for #watchIncident and verify watching is "true" and watchStatus is "Watching incident" after one click.
verificationChecklist: Inspect the rendered incident card with a screen reader or accessibility inspector and confirm the star control is announced as "Watch incident, button".
Click the star button once and confirm the status changes from "Not watching" to "Watching incident" without any altered interaction behavior.
Run the generated regression to confirm both the accessible name and existing watch-state result.
